content.question: Would abolishing the 6-/8-year gimnázium tracks reduce overall educational sorting and segregation, or would it relocate into private schools, within-school classroom sorting, church-school enrollment, or residential school segregation—and what is the net distributional effect of such relocation?
content.reduction_path: Conduct a quasi-experimental or natural-experiment study of a Hungarian region or cohort that removed formal 6-/8-year tracks; compare the subsequent distribution of students across within-school tracks, private/church schools, and residential mobility, and measure whether overall SES/ethnic segregation changed, declined, or shifted in form.
